elements.title: Nellie Breen
elements.contents: ... that Nellie Breen (pictured) is believed to have performed the first tap dance broadcast on radio in 1922?

elements.title: Jaxson Davis
elements.contents: ... that American basketball player Jaxson Davis was cradled by Kobe Bryant when he was an infant?

elements.title: Waverly
elements.contents: ... that Waverly is one of the largest surviving domestic residences in London, Ontario?

elements.title: men's 73 kg weightlifting competition at the 2024 Summer Olympics
elements.contents: ... that the gold-medal favourite for the men's 73 kg weightlifting competition at the 2024 Summer Olympics was eliminated in the last qualifying tournament?

elements.title: Meng Qingshu
elements.contents: ... that Meng Qingshu married Wang Ming only one day after she was released from prison?

elements.title: Something Worth Waiting For
elements.contents: ... that a song from Friko's Something Worth Waiting For was inspired by the Albuquerque International Balloon Fiesta?

elements.title: Perrine Dugué
elements.contents: ... that Perrine Dugué's brutal murder and the rumoured miraculous healing powers of her grave led to the creation of a popular cult in 1796?

elements.title: Nilotica shea butter
elements.contents: ... that Nilotica shea butter, found only in the Nile basin of Uganda, represents less than five percent of the global shea butter supply?

elements.title: Agreeee
elements.contents: ... that the developer of Agreeee wanted to use the game to "draw people's attention to the fact that nobody reads the terms of service"?
